meta_title: Impact Windows in Melrose Park, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs HVHZ-rated impact windows in Melrose Park, FL. 170mph protection, insurance savings & $0 down financing. A+ BBB. Call today!
h1: Impact Windows in Melrose Park, FL — HVHZ-Grade Protection for Your Home
intro:
Melrose Park is a close-knit, established neighborhood tucked within the heart of Broward County, where working families have put down roots for generations. With an average home value of around $300,000 and more than half of the community's housing stock built before 1994, the majority of homes here were constructed before Florida's building codes were dramatically tightened in the wake of Hurricane Andrew. That means a large share of Melrose Park's single-family homes still have standard single-pane or older aluminum windows — windows that were never engineered to withstand what South Florida's hurricane seasons are now known to deliver.

Located in ZIP code 33312 and positioned approximately five miles from the Atlantic coast, Melrose Park sits firmly within Broward County's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — the most demanding wind-load designation in the entire state of Florida. The HVHZ classification means that any window replacement or new installation must meet the strictest product-performance and installation standards outlined in the Florida Building Code. This isn't a technicality — it reflects the genuine meteorological reality of living in one of the most storm-active counties in the United States. With a design wind speed of 170 miles per hour, the forces that can bear down on homes near Fort Lauderdale, Dania Beach, and Davie are extraordinary.

For homeowners in Melrose Park, upgrading to impact-resistant windows isn't simply about checking a code compliance box. It's about protecting your family, your investment, and your peace of mind every time a tropical system forms in the Atlantic or Gulf. Impact windows replace vulnerable glass with laminated, multi-layer panels that resist shattering, prevent wind-driven rain intrusion, and eliminate the need for hurricane shutters altogether. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — South Florida's Kings of Service — has been protecting homes just like yours throughout Broward County, and we bring that same expertise, precision, and commitment to every home we work on in Melrose Park.

section_why.h2: Why Melrose Park Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties not by cutting corners, but by doing things the right way — every time. With over 60 years of combined installation experience among our team, we understand exactly what it takes to properly protect a home in the High Velocity Hurricane Zone. We've completed more than 500 homes throughout South Florida, and our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau reflects a track record of quality that speaks for itself.

One of the most important things that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart from other window companies operating in the Melrose Park area is our 100% in-house installation model. We do not subcontract our labor. Every technician who shows up at your door is a trained, vetted Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employee who understands HVHZ installation standards, local permitting requirements, and product specifications inside and out. In a market flooded with companies that use rotating crews of independent contractors, our in-house approach means consistent quality, direct accountability, and a smoother experience from the first consultation to the final inspection.

For Melrose Park homeowners — many of whom are making a significant financial decision relative to their home's value — this matters enormously. We don't treat your project like a number on a clipboard. We understand that a $300,000 home in this community represents years of hard work and real financial priority, and we approach every installation with the care and attention it deserves. Our team is fully licensed and insured, and we handle the entire permitting process with the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division on your behalf, so you're never left navigating bureaucratic paperwork alone.

From the initial measurement and product selection to pulling permits, completing the installation, and passing the final inspection, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire process. We're also proud to offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available, making HVHZ-grade impact window protection accessible to households across Melrose Park's lower-middle income community without requiring a large upfront investment.

section_history.h2: A Storm History That Proves Why Melrose Park Homes Need Impact Windows
section_history.body:
Broward County has been struck by at least 10 named storms since 1990, and the Melrose Park community has lived through some of the most destructive hurricanes in modern Florida history. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 reshaped how the entire state thought about building codes — its 165-mph winds devastated communities throughout South Florida and exposed just how inadequate standard construction and window glazing truly was. While Andrew's direct impact was centered in Miami-Dade, the storm sent a clear message throughout Broward County: older homes with conventional windows were dangerously vulnerable.

Then came Hurricane Wilma in 2005 — a storm that made a rare west-to-east traverse directly across Broward County, catching many residents off guard with its speed and sustained winds. Wilma caused widespread damage to roofs and windows across communities including the areas surrounding Melrose Park, leaving homeowners without power for weeks and facing costly repairs. More than a decade later, Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced the same lesson, bringing sustained tropical-storm-force winds and gusts well into hurricane territory across the Fort Lauderdale metro area, with reported damage to older windows, screens, soffits, and roofing systems throughout the 33312 ZIP code area.

The pattern is unmistakable: homes with pre-1994 construction — which represent approximately 57% of Melrose Park's housing stock — were built before Florida's modern impact-resistant standards existed, and they remain the most exposed during storm events. Every hurricane season is a reminder that Melrose Park homeowners deserve modern protection. Impact windows are the single most effective upgrade you can make to fortify your home against the storms that South Florida's history tells us will come again.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code & HVHZ Requirements for Impact Windows in Melrose Park
section_code.body:
Melrose Park falls within Broward County's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, which triggers the most rigorous set of building code requirements in the state. Under the Florida Building Code — specifically the High Velocity Hurricane Zone provisions — any impact window installed in this area must be tested and certified to withstand large missile impact, cyclic pressure loading, and water infiltration at extreme wind pressures. Products must carry approval documentation demonstrating compliance with these standards, and installations must follow precise anchoring and sealing specifications that differ significantly from non-HVHZ counties.

Permits for impact window replacement in Melrose Park are processed through the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division, which oversees permitting for unincorporated areas and communities in this section of Broward County. The permit process includes plan review, product approval verification, and a post-installation inspection to confirm that the work meets HVHZ standards. This is not an optional formality — unpermitted window installations can create serious problems when selling your home, filing an insurance claim, or dealing with storm damage assessments. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles this entire permitting process on your behalf, submitting the correct documentation and coordinating inspections so the job is fully code-compliant from start to finish.

The 170-mph design wind speed that governs the Melrose Park area means that every product we install must be rated and tested for those specific performance thresholds. Both ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI products that we install carry the necessary Florida Product Approvals for HVHZ applications, giving you confidence that your new windows aren't just aesthetically upgraded — they are legally and structurally qualified to protect your home according to the highest standards Florida law requires.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: Purpose-Built for South Florida's HVHZ Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading impact window lines in Melrose Park: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are engineered specifically for Florida's demanding coastal and near-coastal environment, with Florida Product Approvals that qualify them for High Velocity Hurricane Zone installation. Whether your home has a traditional architectural style or a more contemporary design, we have product options in both lines that deliver the structural performance your home requires while complementing its existing aesthetic.

ESW impact windows feature multi-chamber extruded aluminum frames that distribute impact forces and wind loads across a wider structural profile, laminated safety glass with an interlayer that holds glass fragments in place even when cracked, and Low-E coatings that significantly reduce solar heat gain — an important feature in South Florida's intense sun. The result is a window that doesn't just resist hurricanes; it also reduces cooling costs, cuts UV exposure to furniture and flooring, and improves the overall comfort of your living space throughout the year. CWI products offer a comparable level of storm performance with design options that work particularly well for homeowners looking to update the visual profile of older Melrose Park homes built in the 1970s and 1980s.

Both product lines meet the large missile impact test requirements mandated under the Florida Building Code's HVHZ provisions, meaning they are tested against nine-pound 2x4 lumber projectiles traveling at wind-borne speeds. This testing standard far exceeds what standard tempered or double-pane windows can withstand. For Melrose Park homeowners concerned about break-ins as well as storms, the laminated glass in both ESW and CWI windows provides a meaningful secondary benefit — the interlayer resists forced entry in ways that conventional glass simply cannot.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost in Melrose Park — And How to Finance Them
section_cost.body:
For a typical Melrose Park home valued around $300,000, the cost of a full impact window replacement will vary based on the number of windows, opening sizes, and specific product configurations. Most single-family homes in this area invest between $8,000 and $18,000 for a complete window replacement project. While this represents a meaningful investment for lower-middle income households, it's important to understand both the financial return and the risk-reduction value that comes with making this upgrade.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing solutions designed specifically to make this protection accessible to Melrose Park homeowners without requiring large cash outlays. Our financing options start at just $87 per month, and we offer $0 down programs for qualified applicants. Spreading the cost over time means you can begin enjoying hurricane protection, energy savings, and insurance discounts immediately — rather than waiting years to accumulate the full cash amount. In many cases, the monthly savings on energy bills and insurance premiums can meaningfully offset the monthly financing cost, making the net out-of-pocket impact much more manageable than the sticker price suggests.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact windows are one of the highest-ROI upgrades available to South Florida homeowners. Real estate professionals consistently note that HVHZ-compliant impact windows are a key selling point in Broward County, and buyers purchasing homes in Melrose Park and nearby Fort Lauderdale increasingly expect impact protection as a baseline feature rather than a luxury upgrade. Installing impact windows now adds demonstrable market value to your property while reducing your annual insurance costs — a combination that makes the financial case compelling regardless of whether you plan to stay in your home for years or eventually put it on the market.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Melrose Park Homeowners Who Install Impact Windows
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing impact windows in Melrose Park is the reduction in homeowners insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market — particularly in Broward County — has been under significant strain in recent years, with many carriers raising rates or tightening coverage terms. However, one consistent way homeowners can push back against rising premiums is by completing a wind mitigation upgrade, and impact windows are among the most impactful changes you can make.

When you install qualifying impact-resistant windows throughout your home, you become eligible for a Wind Mitigation Inspection Report, which documents your home's improved storm-resistance features for your insurance carrier. In Broward County, where wind mitigation credits are governed by Florida statute, insurers are required to offer discounts that reflect verified improvements to your home's ability to withstand wind events. Depending on your carrier and current premium structure, Melrose Park homeowners have reported savings ranging from several hundred to over a thousand dollars annually after completing impact window installations. Common carriers operating in this area — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and others — apply these discounts through a standardized credits system tied directly to the Wind Mitigation Report findings.

Over a ten-year period, the cumulative insurance savings alone can represent a significant portion of your original installation investment. When combined with reduced energy costs from Low-E glass coatings and the elimination of hurricane shutter purchase, storage, and deployment costs, the total financial picture for impact windows in Melrose Park is far more favorable than the initial price tag implies. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team can walk you through the expected savings based on your specific home and current policy so you can make a fully informed decision.

faqs.q: Do I really need HVHZ-rated impact windows if I live in Melrose Park, which is several miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Yes. Melrose Park's location in Broward County places it squarely within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone regardless of its distance from the coast. The HVHZ designation is based on regional wind speed data and storm history — not just proximity to water. The 170-mph design wind speed applies to your home, and any window installation must meet HVHZ certification standards. Distance from the coastline reduces storm surge risk but does not reduce wind exposure in this zone.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost for a typical Melrose Park home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Most Melrose Park homeowners invest between $8,000 and $18,000 for a full impact window replacement, depending on the number and size of windows. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified applicants. Our team will provide a detailed, transparent quote during your free in-home consultation so you know exactly what to expect before making any commitment.

faqs.q: Who handles the building permit for impact window installation in Melrose Park?
faqs.a: Impact window permits in Melrose Park are processed through the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — including documentation submission, product approval verification, and coordination of the post-installation inspection. You don't need to navigate the permit office yourself. We ensure every installation is fully code-compliant and properly closed out so there are no issues if you sell your home or file an insurance claim.

faqs.q: How long does it take to complete an impact window installation in Melrose Park?
faqs.a: The typical timeline from signed contract to completed installation runs four to eight weeks for most Melrose Park homes. This includes the permitting process with the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division, product fabrication to your specific window dimensions, and scheduling the installation. The physical installation for an average-sized home can usually be completed in one to two days by our in-house crew. We'll provide a project timeline during your consultation so you know what to expect at each stage.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowners insurance by installing impact windows in Melrose Park?
faqs.a: Savings vary by carrier and current policy, but Broward County homeowners who install qualifying impact windows and complete a Wind Mitigation Inspection often see annual premium reductions ranging from a few hundred dollars to over $1,000 depending on their insurer and coverage level. Florida law requires insurers to apply wind mitigation credits when documented improvements are verified. Over time, these savings can offset a significant portion of your installation cost, especially when combined with energy savings from Low-E coated glass.

cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for your free, no-obligation consultation — our in-house experts will assess your home, explain your HVHZ options, and provide transparent pricing with flexible financing starting at $87/month.
